reason: Both correctly land on "fix it, don't kill it" with the ~2.2x LTV:CAC and ~6-8 month payback reasoning, and both give reasonable, prioritized CAC-fix tactics (negatives, broad match cleanup, landing page/tracking issues). B goes further in hitting the specified key points: it explicitly calls out branded-vs-generic split, explicitly raises the blended-vs-paid CAC question as a caveat that could flip the decision, and adds a sharp, correct derived insight (LTV/price implies ~13-month average lifetime, i.e. ~7-8% monthly churn) that reframes the real bottleneck as retention rather than acquisition — a genuinely useful, non-obvious catch. A's unique strength is explicitly naming SEO/outbound/partnerships as comparison channels for the same budget, which B omits, but that's one point versus several B covers more thoroughly. Neither has factual errors; both are appropriately concise. On balance B covers more of the decision-relevant substance without padding.
